title: Avoid wrapping collection creation with type constructors
description: Using type constructors (e.g., list(), dict(), set()) to create collections with literals or comprehensions can make code less readable and less type-safe.
message: Avoid using type constructors for collection creation with literals or comprehensions.
recommendation: Use assignment directly or use a library like dataclasses for better type-safety.

noncompliant.description: Creates a list using a list constructor and a literal.
Creates a dictionary using a dictionary constructor and a tuple of key-value pairs.

compliant.description: Creates a list using assignment directly.
Creates a dictionary using assignment directly.

why_is_this_an_issue: Using type constructors can make code less readable and less type-safe.
how_to_fix: Use assignment directly or use a library like dataclasses for better type-safety.

metadata.title: Avoid wrapping collection creation with type constructors
